name: Fuente del Caño Trail
name_original: Ruta de la Fuente del Caño — circular desde Bolaño de Calatrava
description: This difficult 18.8-mile (30.2 km) hike near Bolaños de Calatrava takes you past the San Isidro Hermitage and Fuente del Caño Cave.
region_intro: Hiking around Bolaños De Calatrava offers routes through a landscape shaped by volcanic origins, particularly within the Campo de Calatrava region. The terrain features small Paleozoic mountain ranges like the Sierra del Moral and El Pardillo, alongside shallow creeks such as the Pellejero and de Cuetos. Hikers will encounter diverse vegetation, including holm oaks, vineyards, olive groves, and cereal fields. The area also includes the Fuente del Caño, a significant natural spring at over 800 meters altitude. Best hiking trails around Bolaños De Calatrava The most popular hiking route is Fuente del Caño Trail, an 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail that takes 8 hours to complete. This route offers views of vineyards, olive groves, and volcanic formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita del Calvario – Virgen del Monte loop from Bolaños de Calatrava, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This loop provides varied scenery starting directly from Bolaños de Calatrava. Local hikers also love the Silo – Bullring loop from Almagro, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the area around Almagro, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Hiking around Bolaños De Calatrava is defined by volcanic terrain, small mountain ranges, and diverse agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 7 reviews. Nearly 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Bolaños De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Bolaños de Calatrava?
A: The landscape around Bolaños de Calatrava is distinctively shaped by its volcanic origins, particularly within the Campo de Calatrava region. You'll encounter small Paleozoic mountain ranges like the Sierra del Moral and El Pardillo, alongside shallow creeks. The terrain often features a mix of quartzite sierras, holm oak forests, vineyards, olive groves, and cereal fields.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Bolaños de Calatrava?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes around Bolaños de Calatrava. These range in difficulty, offering options for various experience levels, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance trails.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bolaños de Calatrava offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silo – Bullring loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6 km trail, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave loop from Umbría del Halcón&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 5.5 km and also rated easy.

Q: What are the most scenic natural features to look out for on a hike?
A: A significant natural feature is the Fuente del Caño, a spring located in the Sierra del Guijo, which flows almost year-round and forms the Arroyo del Caño. The area is also rich in volcanic formations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, which contribute to the unique landscape. You'll also see diverse vegetation including moss, thyme, rosemary, and gorse.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31684150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Calvario – Virgen del Monte loop from Bolaños de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 10.3 km. For an easier loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33744185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave loop from Moral de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Bolaños de Calatrava?
A: Given the climate of Castile-La Mancha, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Bolaños de Calatrava?
A: Many trails in natural areas across Spain are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and agricultural areas. It's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, but generally, you should be able to enjoy the trails with your dog.

Q: What are some interesting historical or cultural sites near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you might encounter historical elements within the landscape. The region has a rich history, including ancient Roman settlements. Nearby, you can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique architecture, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: For many trails, especially those starting from or near towns like Bolaños de Calatrava or Almagro, you can typically find parking in designated areas within the town or at specific trail access points. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking options.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bolaños de Calatrava?
A: The hiking routes in Bolaños de Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from numerous reviews. Hikers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the diverse agricultural scenery, and the variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the region?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201414604/fuente-del-cano-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a significant option. This route spans approximately 30.2 km and is rated as difficult, offering an extensive exploration of the area's vineyards, olive groves, and volcanic formations.
tips: Hermitage where the Hermandad de San Isidro Labrador celebrates acts in honor of the agricultural patron. It is very close to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Monte.

Impressive grotto through which water emanates naturally and from which the Arroyo del Caño starts. An official hiking route starts from Bolaños de Cva., along the arranged road to the Virgen del Monte, and from there it continues to the Sierra de Moral. From Bolaños there are 30km there and back, and 20km if you do it from the Virgen del Monte (approximately). It is a somewhat long walk to walk, so I have done it by bike but knowing that the paths that go up to the Caño are narrow, with stones and quite steep. They cannot be done by bike, but I think it is a good option to do the route from Bolaños by bike if you assume that you will have to pick it up a few times and walk with it for a few kilometers along the crest of the mountain range. What an adventure 😅

At the mouth of the cave you can see Bolaños and Almagro.
In summer there is only a puddle of water, but it is spectacular. I suppose that in spring it will take a little more water.

The Sanctuary of the Virgen del Monte is a complex in which the Marian image of the Virgen del Monte is venerated, in the municipality of Bolaños de Calatrava. It consists of two hermitages, a primitive one from the thirteenth century and a later one from the end of the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.

Greenway that joins Bolaños de Cva. with the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Monte.

Pleasant route, well arranged, illuminated, with unevenness and 7km between the round trip, which runs parallel to the cart used by cars.

It is also the beginning of the PR-CR 46 route (Fuente del Caño Route).

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Fuente del Caño Trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It is a long hike, covering over 30 kilometers, and requires a good level of fitness and stamina to complete the full circular route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Caño Trail is estimated to take approximately 8 hours to complete. This duration is for the full 30.2 km circular route and can vary based on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Fuente del Caño Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features several notable highlights. You'll pass by the San Isidro Hermitage, the intriguing Fuente del Caño Cave, and the Virgen del Monte. Part of the route also follows the Greenway from Bolaños to the Virgen del Monte.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente del Caño Trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Fuente del Caño Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given its 'difficult' rating and long distance, ensure your dog is accustomed to long hikes and that you carry enough water for both of you. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and respect other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the long distance more challenging, while winter might bring colder conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Fuente del Caño Trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Fuente del Caño Trail itself. However, always be mindful of local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Fuente del Caño Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Bolaños de Calatrava. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Bolaños de Calatrava, near the starting point of the route. Specific parking areas are not detailed, so look for public parking options in the town.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Fuente del Caño Trail largely follows the official 'Ruta de la Fuente del Caño' for about 97% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the 'Motilla del Azuer - Moral de Calatrava' and the 'Rurta entre santuarios. Santuario de la Virden del Monte. Santuario de Ntra. Sra. de las Nieves' trails.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Fuente del Caño Trail?
faqs.answer: As a long and difficult hike, expect varied terrain. While specific details for this exact trail are not provided, similar routes in the region can include natural paths, dirt tracks, and potentially some uneven or rocky sections. Sturdy hiking shoes are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente del Caño Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Caño Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general location, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing. A map or GPS device (like the komoot app) is also advisable, especially for a long route like this.
